summary:
The patient exhibits symmetric polyarthralgia affecting the proximal interphalangeal and metacarpophalangeal joints consistent with calcium pyrophosphate deposition disease (CPPD). Elevated transferrin saturation and ferritin suggest underlying hemochromatosis, a recognized cause of CPPD via dysregulated metal ion homeostasis leading to crystalline deposits within articular cartilage.  

This summary adheres strictly to clinically significant findings without introducing unsupported assumptions beyond what was provided.
